programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark appears on a game show in a bid to unmask the killer responsible for murdering a previous contestant. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark's old friend, jazz singer Danielle Marsh, visits and begins exhibiting signs of erratic behaviour. However, the doctor is soon alerted to the fact that someone is trying to kill her. Drama, guest starring Helen Reddy, with Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Medical student Alex asks Mark to help clear an old friend mixed up in the murder of a Los Angeles madam, while Madison Wesley is stalked by a killer who has recently undergone plastic surgery. Dick Van Dyke and Joanna Cassidy star(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Madison moves in with her new love, unaware of his true identity and murderous intentions. Meanwhile, Jesse falls under suspicion of writing a scandalous novel revealing spicy details about hospital workers. Starring Joanna Cassidy and Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps Cabe when he receives a distress code from his ex-wife Rebecca. She fears she is being targeted for stumbling upon a money trail, which has links to the assassination of a congressman. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A jet carrying a classified cloaking radar device goes down over Bosnia, and the team helps Navy Seals to find the aircraft so they can recover the technology and locate the pilot's remains. Meanwhile, Walter is forced to bring his sister to meet the team after she has a drunken run-in with the police. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is(n)

programme.desc: A woman is convinced her friend's husband is trying to kill her after she discovers he is a bigamist, but when he is found dead, she's the prime suspect.(n)

Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.desc: The team races against the clock to save a boy who is trapped inside a sinkhole. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Camille Guaty(n)

Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team races against the clock to save a boy who is trapped inside a sinkhole. programme.title: Ms Fisher's Modern Murder Mysteries

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When a French diplomat to a secret international meeting is found dead, Murdoch is drafted in to temporarily impersonate him - but political and sexual intrigue complicate matters(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: A drug dealer dies while in police custody and Sam suspects a cover-up within the unit. He orders a full investigation, though it seems none of the other officers is prepared to help. To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. John Simm and Philip Glenister star(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a bookies' clerk leads the team to 29-year-old Vic Tyler - who Sam is shocked to discover is his own father. He realises the time when he and his mother were abandoned is coming up and desperately tries to persuade the small-time gambler not to leave his wife and child. Meanwhile, Gene sees an opportunity to bring down a crime syndicate(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The old poem about the boy standing on the burning deck takes on new meaning as Kavanagh defends two Navy officers accused of arson, and finds he cannot use his only defence. It is a tough case, and the last thing he needs is extra stress at home - but that does not stop the children planning an anniversary party without telling him. With Andy Serkis, Ray Winstone and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The siege at the hospital takes a turn for the worse as Jack and Neil are taken hostage at gunpoint after an abortive attempt to free Roger. New DC Jo Masters takes charge of the delicate negotiations, while Steve realises only he can save Honey. Elsewhere, Samantha is appalled to learn Abi is carrying Hugh Wallis's baby and Terry begins to doubt whether his brother is guilty of the child abuse charges(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Samantha begins to feel she's trapped in a never-ending nightmare when a confrontation with Abi leads to yet another revelation - she intends to marry her erstwhile kidnapper Hugh. Turning to Phil for help, Samantha hatches a plan to snatch her pregnant, brainwashed daughter from the psychopath's clutches - but it seems she may have underestimated the extent of his cunning. Community Support Officers Laura Bryant and Colin Fairfax are assigned to Sun Hill, where they are greeted by a contemptuous outburst from Dale, and Honey oversteps the line when she attempts to console a distraught husband whose wife has gone missing(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Hugh Wallis turns the tables on Sam Nixon by dropping in at Sun Hill and accusing her of abducting her own daughter. As the tug of love moves toward a dramatic conclusion, Sam desperately tries to make Abi realise she has been brainwashed - and Jack Meadows isn't as supportive as he might be. Elsewhere, Scott Burnett's wife is found murdered and Honey Harmon oversteps the bounds of duty when she attempts to comfort the grieving widower. Drama, starring Lisa Maxwell and Georgia Moffett(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sam struggles to prove that Hugh Wallis abducted, brainwashed and raped her daughter, but the suspect arrogantly sticks to his story that Abi instigated the sexual relationship that has left her pregnant and emotionally wrecked. DCs Jo Masters and Suzie Sim investigate a case involving rent boys and pornography which implicates DI Neil Manson's father-in-law, a Deputy Assistant Commissioner at Scotland Yard and, undaunted by the risk of making enemies in high places, Jack Meadows organises a raid on the officer's home. Honey Harman confesses her unprofessional behaviour to Steve Hunter, but her next anguished encounter with Scott Burnett leads to another tender embrace. Drama, starring Lisa Maxwell and Robert Gwilym(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The die-hard dame of the Wainthropp Detective Agency sets about flushing out a missing company director who is sought by his creditors. Back at home, Hetty's interfering brother-in-law Frank has come to stay and Robert has flown off to Australia to visit their son Derek(n)

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning writer Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel, first published in 1815. Romola Garai stars as the independent Emma Woodhouse, who believes she is a skilled matchmaker and tries to pair up her friends and acquaintances. She takes Harriet Smith under her wing and encourages the woman to set her sights higher than her current suitor - against Mr Knightley's warnings about the dangers of meddling. With Jonny Lee Miller, Michael Gambon, Jodhi May and Tamsin Greig(n)

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: While continuing her efforts to set up Harriet and the vicar, Emma looks forward to meeting Frank Churchill at the Christmas party, but when he fails to show up she becomes the subject of Mr Elton's unwanted attention. Later, village gossip focuses on the arrival of Jane Fairfax and a piano she was given by a mystery admirer, leaving the matchmaker reluctant to believe Mr Knightley could have sent the gift. Drama, starring Romola Garai, Louise Dylan, Blake Ritson and Laura Pyper(n)

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The heroine plans a ball with Frank, and wonders whether she has feelings for him. Despite having a wonderful time at the event, she still feels trapped, and with the matchmaking effort officially abandoned, becomes increasingly bored. However, her spirits are raised by Mr Knightley's proposal for a group outing to Box Hill, where he tells her he thinks Frank and Jane are in love. Drama, starring Romola Garai and Michael Gambon(n)

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The outing to Box Hill ends in disaster when Emma insults Miss Bates. After being berated by Knightley, she realises her behaviour was shameful and tries to make amends, but Jane refuses to speak to her. When Frank's overbearing aunt dies, the Westons expect him to propose to Emma, but his subsequent actions start a chain of events that shocks the heroine, resulting in the realisation of a truth she previously dismissed. Romola Garai and Jonny Lee Miller star in the final instalment of Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A Christian Scientist couple refuse medicine for their newborn baby, leaving Sister Julienne questioning the nature of absolute faith. However, the case becomes more complicated when the parents are accused of harming their child. Barbara uses her initiative to communicate with a pregnant Sylheti woman who cannot speak English, although using the patient's young son to translate provides new problems. Sister Mary Cynthia returns to Nonnatus House and a woman catches handyman Fred's eye(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The daughter of a man held responsible for a fatal boating accident on the Thames comes forward with new evidence, claiming that a property developer sabotaged the vessel. The case means putting Strickland's wealthy sailing friends under the spotlight, much to the team's delight. Guest starring Barbara Flynn (Cranford)(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a body is found half-buried in a swamp. Visiting her home, Wallander discovers signs of an intruder and that the dead woman's daughter is missing. The return of a lost love distracts him from the case at hand, as he encounters a gang of bikers that have been causing trouble in the area and begins to suspect he is being watched(n)

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Drama about military police and their families faced with political and personal conflicts in a 1960s British colony on the Red Sea coast. An officer and his wife arrive in Aden, where he is to replace a much-loved captain. He soon has to adjust to life on the frontline, along with resentment among the men that he was appointed instead of the already-present Lt Ed Laithwaite. Honor is taken under the wing of Laithwaite's wife, Alison, who had been having an affair with Captain Page. Starring Jessie Buckley and Jeremy Neumark Jones(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: Angel and Tess are on their honeymoon when he decides it is time for them to admit their former transgressions, but while she forgives his, he is appalled by what his new wife tells him and heads off to Brazil to set up a farm there by himself. She then finds a job on a bleak farm run by Groby and bumps into Alec, who claims he is a changed man and proposes marriage. Gemma Arterton stars(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A reluctant Brian enters an addiction centre run by monks to treat his alcoholism, but soon becomes bored and looks for a distraction. He finds one when a chance remark leads the team to reopen an investigation into the death of a heroin user nine years previously - and to help reveal the truth, Gerry is sent undercover as a sex addict. Richard Wilson and Fay Ripley guest star(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack examines a case previously undertaken by a deceased colleague, who was convinced that the alleged suicide of a journalist was a cover-up by the American government. Enlisting the help of a UFO-spotter, the team discovers the reporter witnessed a mysterious air crash near a US Air Force base, and the retired officers try to track down the original personnel to get some answers. Guest starring Mark Williams, Glynis Barber, Michael Brandon and Raquel Cassidy, with Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Drysdale decides to send the boys on an escort mission to Africa, but it proves to be a fiasco on every level - particularly for Jeremy, who first refuses to follow the CO's orders to the letter, then has a bitter reunion with ex-wife Lilian that sets the sparks flying. Mark also resurfaces, as a mercenary soldier fighting for the enemy and keen to gain revenge on his former King's Own colleagues(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sheelagh arrests a man accused of deliberately infecting women with HIV, but Leela reckons her colleague is prejudiced against the colour of the suspect's skin - and when she learns Sheelagh was demoted to PC because of a racism allegation, it seems her worst fears are confirmed. Jim is appalled when his ex-wife's decomposing body is found, and Neil is faced with hard evidence of his father-in-law's fondness for rent boys(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Diane's anxiety over the coroner's inquest into Dominic's death is compounded by feelings of nausea, and Ric suggests her physical symptoms might mean she's pregnant. Matt and Dean compete for the attention of a patient's daughter, but a hung-over Mubbs has no time for their messing around. However, he's grateful for the help when they stop him making a serious mistake(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Mark and Eve try to make good impressions as Christmas nears, motivated by possible promotions in the pipeline. Baz and Charlie harbour hostile feelings towards each other, and matters come to a head at the department's party when Elliot asks the pair to lead them into a slow dance(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Leela and Sheelagh find themselves at odds over the suspicious death of a baby when it seems accusations of abuse directed at the infant's asylum-seeker parents could be fuelled by racism. Honey accuses Amber of trying to ruin her relationship with Steve - then displays breathtaking hypocrisy by falling into bed with Scott Burnett. Neil's day goes from bad to worse as Phil taunts him about his disgraced father-in-law and Andrea returns to the station, only to plant a kiss on Smithy(n)

programme.desc: Cockroaches at the Meal Machine threaten Ian's success. Rachel's patience is threatened by her mother's surprise visit, and Pauline comes home to find her family gone(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.desc: Stuart 'Recall' McKenzie joins Blue Watch, and there are a series of incidents involving schoolchildren on their half-term break(n)

Unfortunately the deal backfires, and he's forced to use every ounce of his ingenuity to ensure they both emerge unscathed. Frank Finlay guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.desc: Jim finds himself the target of a ruthless villain when he tries to help Charlie with a problem from his past. John Nettles and Terrance Alexander star(n)

programme.desc: Joe meets up with Roxy but her husband is not pleased to see him. Mr Boswell and Lilo Lill are in a watchman's hut when an accident occurs(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted has a chance of stardom when he is hired as a late replacement at an uptown show for a fellow comedian. It is rumoured that talent scouts from the TV show Opportunity Knocks will be in the audience. Classic comedy following life at Maplin's holiday camp, starring Paul Shane, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel's script makes it onto American TV, but the show gets a mixed reception. The couple make a wise decision in refusing Alistair's invitation to enjoy New York hospitality. Comedy, starring Geoffrey Palmer and Judi Dench(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

As ever, the two design teams go head to head, so it remains to be seen whther newcomer Arit's garden, which uses repeating circle motifs to create interest in an otherwise small and featureless space and incorporates a curved wooden seat surrounded by bright flowers, will triumph(n)

programme.desc: Lord Farquaad returns as a ghost and is just as evil as ever. He kidnaps Fiona, meaning Shrek and Donkey must set out to save her - with a little help from Dragon, who ate the villainous royal in the first place. Spin-off from the Shrek movies(n)

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher meets Essex farmer David Eagle, who has let the sea inundate acres and acres of his farmland to create vital saltmarsh habitat for migratory birds(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Graham, Yasmin and Ryan join the Doctor on a trip back to 1816, where they visit the Villa Diodati on the shores of Lake Geneva as Lord Byron and the Shelleys plan a night telling ghost stories. Their aim is to spend the evening soaking up the atmosphere in the presence of some literary greats - but a supernatural presence is all too real and the Doctor is forced into a decision of earth-shattering proportions(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: After performing an excellent forceps delivery in final supervision, Dr McNulty's next patient is a woman whose husband is a drunk and abusive towards her. At the clinic, the team finally receives the long-awaited incubator for the maternity home, while Trixie is keen to support new patient Marion, who has a rare form of retinitis pigmentosa that has left her blind(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Mark is consumed with paranoia, fearful that his life is at risk and that the perpetrator is someone known to him. To make matters worse, Detective Inspector Lejeune seems to be increasingly suspicious of him. Mark's investigations uncover the ties between his first wife Delphine and the trio of 'witches' at Much Deeping, putting his relationship with second wife Hermia under great strain. Concluding part of the adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, which were Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park and Arsenal v Newcastle United at Emirates Stadium. The two north London clubs prevailed in the reverse fixtures on the opening weekend of the campaign and will both have been hopeful of taking the honours once again. Plus, action from yesterday and Friday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.title: Can You Beat the Bookies?
programme.desc: Comedian and football presenter Lloyd Griffith embarks on an experiment to find out if he can uncover the secrets of gambling success and the truth about the industry(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain 400m hurdler Eilidh Doyle, Burnley goalkeeper Nick Pope, netball star Pamela Cookey and England rugby union international Zach Mercer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle witness the giraffes making their way to their summer paddock, while a newborn reindeer calf has become weak and is unable to suckle from its mother(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Today's auction property-developing projects take an incredible 14 days to more than two and a half years to complete. Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander visit the properties in Sandgate in Kent and Bolton in Lancashire as well as a plot of land in Enfield in north London at the start of the projects, following the progress until they're finished(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with the Walters family, who spent a trial week sampling life in Perth two years previously, to discover if they chose to stay in the UK or move to Australia(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers track down those responsible for a crash that scarred an entire community, and raid the home of an illegal money lender who preys on the vulnerable(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Liverpool's St Georges Hall, while Eric Knowles finds out why the building came to be built in the city(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma and Sid oversee an extremely complicated pregnancy, while Emma and Lena sort things out, and Daniel has an unwanted audience(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When a pampered dog runs away from a life of luxury, Frank and Lu are asked to track it down. Comedy drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a house in rural North Yorkshire on a budget of £800,000, and pauses in Harrogate to take afternoon tea(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Goat's milk ice-cream producers Ian and Lauren and cider-makers Shawn and Trudie compete with the weather and the FIFA World Cup at the Great British Food Festival(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Margie Cooper set off on a search for items in the Highlands, heading for an auction showdown in Aberdeen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Jonny Meah asks if keeping exotic pets at home should be banned and Gareth Furby meets families under siege from pimps and prostitutes working on their doorstep. Plus, how one young entrepreneur is making a mint from designer trainers(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kicking off a week of episodes for the soap's 35th anniversary, all taking place on the same day. Linda revels in her divorce from Mick but her happiness is short-lived when she comes to blows with Shirley - and things only gets worse at the Thames boat party when she continues to throw alcohol down her throat and embarrass her family. Patrick grows fed up of Isaac's behaviour towards him and pleads with Sheree to tell him the truth. Sonia is stunned when the police turn up(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation into the multinational company's rise to corporate superpower, asking whether there is a dark side to the public's love affair with the firm. Former high-level insiders reveal that its customer obsession has led to a huge data-gathering operation, enabling the company to use what it knows about us to shape not only the future of retail, but the workplace and technology too. The programme also hears from senior executives who say the company is a force for good(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes becomes concerned when Dermot announces he wants to ask his childhood friend Buster, a known criminal, to be his best man. However, she is distracted when her neighbour Winnie's husband is rushed to hospital, leaving her son trying to raise funds for his wedding by getting embroiled in a money-making scheme(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The film crew arrives back in the village to discover some very sad news about a popular local resident. Meanwhile, Kerry faces some financial difficulties, so Kurtan steps in to help support her. Return of the spoof documentary, written by and starring Daisy May Cooper and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Don't Scream
programme.desc: Yung Filly hosts a game show in which three best friends go through a physical and psychological test in which they lose £100 from the £5,000 prize money every time they scream(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by singer, actor and producer Justin Timberlake and Oscar-nominated actress Anna Kendrick, who are both part of the voice cast of animated sequel Trolls World Tour. Singer-songwriter Sam Smith also appears, alongside comedian Alan Carr and Strictly and The Greatest Dancer star Oti Mabuse. Plus, more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out some mind-boggling armies from the natural world, including some sleepy suburbs invaded by 10 million bugs(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Caitlin wants to become a conservationist when she's older(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Daredevil wildlife presenter Steve Backshall is live in the studio to talk about his career(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team head to Alaska, where they spot humpback whales and grizzly bears(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Food
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and a team of experts examine the cutting-edge technologies and produce transforming how food is grown, bought and eaten around the world. In the first edition, Dara heads to Kent to visit hi-tech greenhouse farm Thanet Earth, which grows a high quantity of fruit and vegetables throughout the year - and all without any soil. Chef Angela Hartnett tackles sell-by dates with the help of the US military, greengrocer Chris Bavin is in Australia to see the world's leading robotic farm and technology expert Shini Somara is in Shanghai to immerse herself in the restaurant of the future(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this documentary following a couple of mountain lion families in Wyoming's Rocky Mountains. The dramatic story of two mothers struggling to raise their cubs is giving scientists an invaluable insight into the behaviour of these elusive predators(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore Anglesey, the largest island in the Irish Sea. Mehreen recalls the story of the Menai Strait's magnificent bridges, while Adrian dons a wet suit to meet local paddleboarding legend Sian Sykes and see how she's playing a key role in helping rid the island of waste plastic. Plus, comedian Tudur Owen fulfils a boyhood dream by joining the Holyhead truckers at their brand new truck stop, Lucy Martin explores the shipwreck that changed the way weather is forecast and wildlife expert Patrick Ayree gets up close to the wild ponies of Newborough Warren(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor and property enthusiast Caroline Quentin explore unique homes in India, including a house built in the highlands outside of Mumbai, and another within the mountainous scenery of the Western Ghats. Elsewhere, the pair also visit a riverside bolthole drawing on Indian architectural heritage, before ending with a unusual building named 'Collage House'(n)

programme.title: Flatpack Empire
programme.desc: One of IKEA's newest designers, Hanna-Kaarina Heikkila, wants to challenge mass production by creating an 'imperfect vase' that has the finger-marks of its creator. She travels to China to see if her idea is possible. There's also a visit to the home of the Billy bookcase to see what it takes to be a supplier for IKEA, as the company tries to pitch a new range of wardrobes(n)

programme.title: Move Over, Darling (1963)
programme.desc: A woman returns home to America after five years marooned on a desert island, wanting to surprise her husband. However, she finds he has not only given her up for dead, but has also remarried and is enjoying a romantic honeymoon. Remake of vintage comedy My Favourite Wife, starring Doris Day, James Garner and Polly Bergen(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An exploration of the romantic comedy, one of the most successful movie genres of all time, featuring interviews with on-screen couples including Doris Day and Rock Hudson, and Meg Ryan and Tom Hanks. The documentary also highlights how the rom-com has, over the years, given many female actors the opportunity to shine over their male co-stars, and looks at the 'Hugh factor' - and the impact of Richard Curtis's Four Weddings and a Funeral on Hollywood romance in the 21st century(n)

programme.title: 10 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Documentary following young dogs and their new owners over the course of their all-important first six months together. Telephone psychic Delia is keen on the idea of a big-breed dog to join her in her London flat, so canine behaviourist Louise Glazebrook arranges for her to try before she buys. Cocker spaniel Poppy joins the Vaughan family in leafy Surrey, where she gets the undivided attention of the children. However, three-year-old Isadora is a little too eager to play, even when the pooch wants to rest, and when Poppy bites back, Louise arrives with some advice on puppies and young children(n)

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Animals that travel in and out of cities in search of food or a safe place for their young. African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Sergeant Wilson inherits a title following the death of an elderly relative and joins the aristocracy - much to Mainwaring's annoyance. Classic comedy first aired in 1973, starring John Le Mesurier and Arthur Lowe(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores British Columbia, discovering how two superpowers nearly came to war over a pig and joining the Royal Canadian Navy to firefight on board the frigate HMCS Regina. He also explores the rich British heritage and colonial past of Victoria - the provincial capital of British Columbia - discovers the origins of the Hudson's Bay Company, helps to carve a 36- foot totem pole and learns how to head off a charging bear(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig finds herself stuck in a 'Quagmire', as she invites Sindhu Vee, Sally Phillips and Aisling Bea to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Gina Yashere comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. She warms up the audience before introducing routines by 2018 Britain's Got Talent winner Lost Voice Guy and Mock the Week regular Ed Gamble(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin finds a way of showing Officer Wright he can get to him, and begins expanding into other areas and working with other gangs - but that brings unpredictability. The CIA have big plans for Teddy's operation in Nicaragua, but his new Colombian contacts have stopped the supply - Teddy will need Julia's help moving out of his comfort zone to get things back on track(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Alton tells Franklin some truths about what he can expect from his involvement with the CIA, so Franklin begins digging into his family history - and into Teddy's. Julia learns the reality of what Teddy has been doing out on his own without the agency's help, while Gustavo is setting everything up at the border(n)

programme.title: Captain Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A father raises his six children off the grid in the forests of the Pacific Northwest, creating a rigorous physical and intellectual process of education for them. He faces the challenge of reintegrating his family into society, a task that forces him to reassess his idea of what it means to be a parent. Comedy drama, starring Viggo Mortensen and Frank Langella(n)

programme.title: First Snow (2006)
programme.desc: A salesman stranded in a remote New Mexico town is told by a psychic that he will die the next day that it snows. As the weather grows colder, he becomes obsessed with revisiting his past as he strives to find a way to change his destiny before it is too late. Thriller, starring Guy Pearce, Piper Perabo, William Fichtner and JK Simmons(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke are in Abingdon, Oxfordshire, helping a mother install wheelchair access and bring a bit of colour to her neglected garden for her young daughter(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: I'm a Celebrity comrades Stanley Johnson and Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo compete to find profit-making antiques in Cumbria, guided by auctioneers Paul Laidlaw and Izzie Balmer. Toff takes time out from shopping to learn about the importance boats have played in the Lake District and Stanley learns about the conversation work of artist John Ruskin(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Carole Baxter present horticultural tips, beginning with a look at gardening on a budget. Mairi Reid from Ardersier talks about her love for gardening and the pleasure she finds in feeding her family for almost no cost. Mairi does not have the space in her own garden to grow food so she has struck up a fruitful and inspirational partnership with friends and neighbours to share land, knowledge and produce(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his gastronomic road trip from Venice to Istanbul taking in the countries of the former Byzantine Empire - a renowned melting pot of East and West. This instalment finds the gastronome in the city of Ravenna, where he discovers Piadinas, a thin Italian flatbread street food dating back to Roman times. He then crosses the Adriatic Sea to Croatia - a first for Rick - and a gets a chance to discover new a wealth of dishes such as pasticada, goat stew with peas and lamb peka(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether yoga can lower your blood pressure as much as a workout, and Alain Gregoire tests meal replacement shakes to see if they are as healthy as they claim. Meanwhile, Zoe Williams discovers why wearing your contact lenses in the shower damage your eyesight, and Giles Yeo tests whether gourmet salts are any healthier than table salt(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Natural history film examining the difficulties faced by Japanese macaques - also known as snow monkeys. Made famous by a photograph published on the cover of Life Magazine in 1970 depicting one of the animals bathing in a hot spring, the primates have become a tourist attraction but face a daily struggle survive in the harsh climes of the mountains. Imagine Glen narrates(n)

programme.title: Painting the Holy Land
programme.desc: Part one of two. Artist Lachlan Goudie follows Jesus's last days on Earth, the events of Holy Week that see him travel from the north of what is now Israel to Jerusalem and the site of the Crucifixion. It's a pilgrimage that millions undertake, and a story of love and suffering that has inspired some of the world's most remarkable masterpieces(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly judge takes TV presenter Dan Walker back to Aberdeen to reminisce about a memorable childhood holiday he enjoyed there in 1991(n)

programme.title: I Was Monty's Double (1958)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about actor and army officer ME Clifton-James, whose remarkable resemblance to General Montgomery saw him selected for a special assignment during the Second World War. He was trained to imitate the general perfectly, and sent on a tour of North Africa to keep the real Montgomery's whereabouts unknown to the enemy. Clifton-James stars as both himself and Monty, alongside John Mills and Cecil Parker(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A compilation edition, featuring finds at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers a fascinating family history in Cornwall(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve leads a team of experts - including world-leading underwater cave explorer Robbie Schmittner, former Royal Marine Aldo Kane and diving camera operator Katy Fraser - into the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico. Under the vast jungle is the largest unexplored network of caves in the world, comprising thousands of kilometres of passageways. They are beautiful but dangerous, as much of the system is under water. First, the team must trek through tough, scorpion-infested jungles to camp alongside a giant sinkhole - a dark gateway to an underworld full of nightmarish creatures(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Jenny Jones meets up with a group of skiers and snowboarders determined to find eco-friendly thrills in a new movie called Shelter. Plus, a round-up of the latest World Cup action(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff tackle the Baja 1000t off-road race. On the test track, a showdown is held between a Renault hot hatch, a Porsche sports car and an old Lamborghini. Guests Laurence and Emilia Fox drop in to the studio(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Three of the couples are taken on a demanding and dangerous trek to Canwell Glacier system, an area that is home to wolves and bears. They face a gruelling journey over rough glacial terrain and icy streams, and soon find evidence that there are wild animals in the vicinity. As the competitors sleep under the stars, Duane and Rena have to decide who the next pair to be eliminated will be(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by Katherine Ryan(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian mingles among the crowds of the Edinburgh Fringe before heading to a bar where he meets author Val McDermid, learning her secret to creating realistic characters and dialogue. Travelling north, Frankie stays the night in a hotel linked to Dracula, prompting him to offer his own theory on what inspired Bram Stoker to create the famous vampire. Then, at Grampian Prison, he joins inmates learning about the Scots language. Finally, he performs his stand-up show in Aberdeen, alongside sign language expert Catherine King whose job it is to interpret his sometimes shocking routines(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Two-part documentary about the investigation and trial of two carers accused of murdering Margaret Fleming and claiming benefits in her name for 16 years, taking audiences to the centre of the trial at Glasgow's High Court and offering a compelling insight into the work of the police and prosecutors in bringing the two suspects to trial(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Part two of two. The trial of Edward Cairney and Avril Jones continues, including the testimonies of Jean McSherry, the former partner of Margaret's father Derick, and Margaret's former teacher Jacqueline Cahill. The programme also follows senior investigating officer Detective Superintendent Paul Livingstone as he revisits Margaret's derelict home in Seacroft, Inverkip(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate in Dundee, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to St Albans, Hertfordshire, to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron plans to give Chloe his mother's necklace as a Valentine's Day gift - but first he needs to get it back from Nicky. She has problems of her own - she's pawned Bernie's necklace to pay her mother's debts, but is still being harassed for more money, forcing her to put her life and career on the line. Sacha is troubled by a personal decision, but receives guidance from an old friend(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Derry-born Ciara McEleney wanted to move to Perth to give her daughters a sunshine lifestyle, but husband Ruairi had big concerns. One year on Nicki Chapman returns to find out what they decided to do(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall hunt for objects to take to auction. Presenter Paul Martin heads to Sewerby Hall in Bridlington to learn the life story of Hull girl Amy Johnson, the first woman to fly solo from England to Australia(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the return of the competition in which 10 home cooks move into a shared house and compete in a series of weekly challenges designed to demonstrate their flair, skill and creativity. There is also a new addition to the judging line-up as Michelin-starred chef Angela Hartnett joins home cooking queen Mary Berry and produce expert Chris Bavin. For their first two challenges, the cooks have to come up with their ultimate pasta dinner and a dish based on a piece of fruit(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig follows in the footsteps of Charles Dickens as she explores the Kent countryside, visiting the setting of Great Expectations and the house where the author died(n)

programme.title: Super Cute Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan embarks on a global journey to find some of the world's cutest animals, aiming to understand why people find them so endearing and revealing the surprising science behind each creature. He begins with the giant panda, whose unusually large head tells a story of millions of years of evolution and survival. Gordon also reveals how the fennec fox's ears are crucial to its survival, discovers the surprising secret behind a penguin's waddle and meets a five-year-old chimpanzee whose giggle reveals new information about man's evolution(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Bridget is back and pursuing repayment of a widow's debt, whiel a nervy Rory takes a risk to protect Laser Byrne, and a trespasser reacts poorly to Paudge(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn relive the golden age of steam. They begin by exploring how the introduction of steam railways in the early 19th century created a domestic revolution, from the way people lived to the food they ate. Arriving at Ffestiniog Railway in Snowdonia, they find out how millions of tons of slate were moved down the mountain, while underground, Alex experiences the brutal conditions face by miners in the Llechwedd Quarry(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown, Gillian Burke and Chris Packham investigate the health of the UK's waters and look at stories featuring marine wildlife. Steve reveals how ocean plastic rubbish is turned into kayaks used to help clean up the seas, Gillian goes oyster fishing in Cornwall and Chris meets author Philip Hoare, who thinks everyone should take a daily dip in the sea(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's rail exploration of South-east Asia continues in Malaysia, along the way learning how rubber engineering ensures railway passengers enjoy a smooth ride(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the best methods to learn a new language, putting the two most popular styles of learning to the test. Twins Des and Tina embark on 30 hours of scheduled lessons with a language teacher, while twins Lia and Nick take on the challenge of self-teaching using apps, but without a teacher it will take dedication and self-discipline for our twins, is this just too much to achieve alone? Elsewhere, sets of twins also test the best ways to lower your calorie intake, and uncover the secrets to looking younger(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of the Munros of Scotland, the music and members of Steely Dan, the life and works of Adam Smith and the films of Pixar Animation Studios. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Forrests take on the Choristers in the second of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Nebuchadnezzar, The Fallen Woman, A Masked Ball, and The Troubadour(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the fourth of the quarter-finals, two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Confronting Holocaust Denial with David Baddiel
programme.desc: The Holocaust is one of the most documented, witnessed and written about events in history, yet one in six people worldwide either think its scale has been exaggerated or deny that it took place. In this film, comedian David Baddiel explores the multi-faceted nature of Holocaust denial - in both historical and contemporary terms - in an attempt to understand what motivates this phenomenon and why it is on the rise, both in Britain and across the globe(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Trevor and Julia always want to make Christmas really special for their family, but this year money is so tight that things are not going to be easy. As each door of the Advent calendar opens, everyone will have to do their bit to make the festive season happen. The latest instalment in the darkly comic anthology written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with guest appearances by Debbie Rush, Gaby French, Bobby Schofield and Olly Hudson-Croker(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Return of the documentary showing the challenges faced by the NHS, focusing on the work of seven trusts across Liverpool, whose hospitals stretch to north Wales, Cheshire and the Isle of Man. The city's new state-of-the-art hospital remains unfinished, three years after it was due to open, so two of the biggest hospital trusts are planning an ambitious merger. But this means that hundreds of patients and staff have to be transported across the city, while all the operating theatres bar one are closed(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the region's islands, which are home to a vast array of extraordinary wildlife. The programme reveals how remote human communities native to the area are surrounded by harsh terrain and subjected to extreme weather conditions. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Cadi and Vaughan are called to investigate when a body is discovered in a house outside Blaenau Ffestiniog. Meanwhile three youngsters, Mia, Connor and Lee, try to return to a normal life they have lost. Return of the crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams and Siôn Alun Davies. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Paris
programme.desc: In the 1960s as a young expatriate Australian writer, Clive James discovered Paris, which he felt was his spiritual home. In this programme he returns to the city and meets the writers, the models and the actresses who were part of a different world in his youth, and also encounters the unchanging arch-enemy of any visitor - the Paris taxi driver(n)

programme.title: The Salesman (2016)
programme.desc: A woman is attacked in her apartment and her husband is determined to find the assailant, despite her objections. Iranian drama, starring Shahab Hosseini and Taraneh Alidoosti(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo, Sybil Ruscoe and Rod McKenzie host the edition first shown on March 16, featuring performances by New Order, Gloria Estefan, Chanelle, Fuzzbox, Madonna, Soul II Soul with Caron Wheeler, Jason Donovan and Guns N' Roses(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane host the edition first shown on March 23, featuring performances by the Reynolds Girls, Alyson Williams, Donna Summer, Coldcut with Lisa Stansfield, Pat and Mick, Roachford, the Bangles, Kym Mazelle, Kim Wilde, Paula Abdul, Madonna and Bobby Brown(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Musician Angela Moran looks at the history of Birmingham's Irish residents, talking to local people and presenting rare archive footage. She discovers the impact that the 1974 pub bombings had on the community, many of whom felt the need to disguise their heritage, and recalls her own experiences of growing up in the 1990s, when being Irish became fashionable(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital(n)

programme.title: Dangerous Earth
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski presents this documentary showing how new camera technology is revealing the inner workings of the Earth's most spectacular natural wonders. The first programme looks at the anatomy of an avalanche, from eyewitness footage from within one to detailed CT scans showing the microscopic changes that cause them(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Snowdonia during the autumn, finding red squirrels on the lower slopes of the Carneddau range and jays collecting acorns in ancient woodland near Aber Falls. He also discovers old sheepfolds and Wales' native black cattle breed on the high slopes - both relics of a time when many more people lived and worked on the uplands and evidence of Snowdonia's changing nature(n)

programme.title: Attenborough: 60 Years in the Wild
programme.desc: David Attenborough reflects on scientific discoveries in his lifetime that have transformed the way the natural world is viewed - including evidence of how and where life began, the ways in which continents move, and research into animal communication and behaviour. He also recalls some of his more hair-raising attempts to bring new science to a television audience, and clips show him standing in the shadow of an erupting volcano and being charged at by a group of armed New Guinea tribesmen(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Michelangelo was not merely a giant of the Renaissance. He was also one of the era's most controversial personalities. Art historian Sona Datta explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Sona reveals how TV has tried to reconcile Michelangelo's art with his difficult personality, bringing to life the story of a man who rose from humble beginnings to favoured artist of the rich and powerful(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Art experts Jacky Klein and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit museums as they track down previously unknown paintings by some of the world's greatest artists. In the first edition, the pair head to a museum store in Swansea, where they reveal a multi-million pound lost masterpiece with the potential to rewrite art history. Plus, Jacky is prompted to re-examine the life of renowned Polish artist Josef Herman, who created a giant painting of local coal miners(n)

programme.title: Armando's Tale of Charles Dickens
programme.desc: Satirist and lifelong Charles Dickens fan Armando Iannucci, worried that endless adaptations of the novelist's work have led his skills to go unappreciated, sets out to prove why he still matters as a writer in the 21st century. Using David Copperfield as a starting point, he unpicks the language and the revolution of a master storyteller, and with the help of funnymen Barry Cryer, Kevin Eldon and Phill Jupitus, he gets beneath the skin of some of the more outlandish characters. Finally, through encounters with the types of people Dickens wrote about, including lawyers and a debtor, Iannucci claims that his stories are just as relevant today(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: The history of a quintessentially British institution, which was formed in 1791, originally for military reasons before becoming a valuable tool in peacetime for ramblers and adventurers. Timeshift examines how the OS is adjusting to the digital age, with the large folding maps now just a small part of its output(n)

programme.title: A Brief History of Graffiti
programme.desc: Dr Richard Clay traces the development of graffiti over 30,000 years, from the prehistoric cave-paintings found in Burgundy, France, through to the gladiatorial fan-worship of Roman Lyon, and the messages left on the walls of the German Reichstag by Russian troops in 1945. Along the way, he unpicks the many reasons that compel people to scribble and scratch lasting mementos to their successors into any available surface, and reveals how these rudimentary memorials have developed into a legitimate art-form(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. This time, she examines the discovery of a 40,000-year-old artwork known as The Lion Man, found in a remote cave in Germany in 1939 by Nazi party member and archaeologist, Robert Wetzel(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Michelangelo was not merely a giant of the Renaissance. He was also one of the era's most controversial personalities. Art historian Sona Datta explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Sona reveals how TV has tried to reconcile Michelangelo's art with his difficult personality, bringing to life the story of a man who rose from humble beginnings to favoured artist of the rich and powerful(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: The Black Swan makes good headway as it enters Greek waters, but disaster strikes when the mainsail rips and the vessel barely reaches the next port - Corfu. Back on dry land, Francesco enjoys a game of cricket and meets David Bellamy, who points him in the direction of the island's animal life(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the Antipodean island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation(n)

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian concludes his review by looking at the last few years of the decade and a society transformed by accelerated change. Not only did the late 1980s see the rise of the yuppie and the economic big bang in the City of London, but shopping habits changed with the growing popularity of German cars, French wine, Italian fashion and Scandinavian interior design. Alongside this transformation was a growing disconnection from the political elite, signified by the growing rave culture and the community charge riots. For Margaret Thatcher, widely seen as the architect of much of this change, things would not end well(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following four Thai-Danish couples living in a small fishing community on the west coast of Denmark where more than 900 Thai women are married to the local fishermen, exploring the universal questions of love and romance(n)

programme.title: Art of Germany
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon investigates the story of German art in the 20th century, which was affected by Adolf Hitler's propaganda against modern pieces before the Second World War, and by a divided nation after the conflict. He explores the work of Otto Dix, George Grosz and the age of the Bauhaus, plus post-war examples by Georg Baselitz, Hilla Becher and Joseph Beuys, to see how art found a place at the heart of the nation after the global battle(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Carl's accordion routines are hindering George's puppet show rehearsals. George must decide whether to keep Carl and risk the show, or replace him and risk his friendship(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep

programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special experts edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Dr Zoe Williams, Dr Hilary Jones, Joe Swift, Rachel De Thame, Francis Wilson, Alex Beresford, Linda Barker and Judi James(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining eight acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby and Violette's relationship is pushed to the limit and Harmony's life is put in danger. Will struggles to finish a tedious report for Connie and turns to Lara for help, but he soon learns he should not mix his personal life with his work. Elsewhere, tensions run high when Ethan and Rash are forced to work together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Norwich City v Liverpool at Carrow Road and Southampton v Burnley at Saint Mary's Stadium. Liverpool will have expected to extend their lead at the top of the table with victory over the struggling Canaries, while the much-improved Saints were looking to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of the Clarets on the opening day of the season. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton and Leicester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Roger Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: James Williams presents the latest political news and debate, as well as reports from around Wales(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience at Leicester Grammar School(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Single mother Zoe from West Yorkshire longs for a new life in Australia for her and her three children, but eldest teenage daughter Courtney does not share her mother's enthusiasm(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Newmarket Racecourse, Suffolk, where two teams of best friends go head to head to maximise their returns, assisted by experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Anita also learns about a legend of horse racing who didn't set foot on the turf(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits St Mary's Kempley in Gloucestershire to see its 12th-century Bible paintings, and finds out about the Chapel of Our Lady In the Crag in North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Monmouthshire countryside to help a couple who have a love of the outdoors and dogs, and hope to buy their first home together with a £700,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Shrek: The Ghost of Lord Farquaad
programme.desc: Lord Farquaad returns as a ghost and is just as evil as ever. He kidnaps Fiona, meaning Shrek and Donkey must set out to save her - with a little help from Dragon, who ate the villainous royal in the first place. Spin-off from the Shrek movies(n)

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Host Tudur Owen relives the best of on-air entertainment, referencing the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of music, magic and karaoke, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher meets Essex farmer David Eagle, who has let the sea inundate acres and acres of his farmland to create vital saltmarsh habitat for migratory birds(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Graham, Yasmin and Ryan join the Doctor on a trip back to 1816, where they visit the Villa Diodati on the shores of Lake Geneva as Lord Byron and the Shelleys plan a night telling ghost stories. Their aim is to spend the evening soaking up the atmosphere in the presence of some literary greats - but a supernatural presence is all too real and the Doctor is forced into a decision of earth-shattering proportions(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: After performing an excellent forceps delivery in final supervision, Dr McNulty's next patient is a woman whose husband is a drunk and abusive towards her. At the clinic, the team finally receives the long-awaited incubator for the maternity home, while Trixie is keen to support new patient Marion, who has a rare form of retinitis pigmentosa that has left her blind(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Mark is consumed with paranoia, fearful that his life is at risk and that the perpetrator is someone known to him. To make matters worse, Detective Inspector Lejeune seems to be increasingly suspicious of him. Mark's investigations uncover the ties between his first wife Delphine and the trio of 'witches' at Much Deeping, putting his relationship with second wife Hermia under great strain. Concluding part of the adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, which were Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park and Arsenal v Newcastle United at Emirates Stadium. The two north London clubs prevailed in the reverse fixtures on the opening weekend of the campaign and will both have been hopeful of taking the honours once again. Plus, action from yesterday and Friday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident(n)

programme.title: Can You Beat the Bookies?
programme.desc: Comedian and football presenter Lloyd Griffith embarks on an experiment to find out if he can uncover the secrets of gambling success and the truth about the industry(n)

programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle witness the giraffes making their way to their summer paddock, while a newborn reindeer calf has become weak and is unable to suckle from its mother(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Catching up with the Walters family, who spent a trial week sampling life in Perth two years previously, to discover if they chose to stay in the UK or move to Australia(n)

programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Liverpool's St Georges Hall, while Eric Knowles finds out why the building came to be built in the city(n)
