programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma and Sid oversee an extremely complicated pregnancy, while Emma and Lena sort things out, and Daniel has an unwanted audience(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When a pampered dog runs away from a life of luxury, Frank and Lu are asked to track it down. Comedy drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a house in rural North Yorkshire on a budget of £800,000, and pauses in Harrogate to take afternoon tea(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Goat's milk ice-cream producers Ian and Lauren and cider-makers Shawn and Trudie compete with the weather and the FIFA World Cup at the Great British Food Festival(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Margie Cooper set off on a search for items in the Highlands, heading for an auction showdown in Aberdeen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Omar Hamdi, Lucy Owen and Rachel Treadaway-Williams present holiday booking tips. Plus, reports on inaccurate scales and a campervan with a damp problem(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kicking off a week of episodes for the soap's 35th anniversary, all taking place on the same day. Linda revels in her divorce from Mick but her happiness is short-lived when she comes to blows with Shirley - and things only gets worse at the Thames boat party when she continues to throw alcohol down her throat and embarrass her family. Patrick grows fed up of Isaac's behaviour towards him and pleads with Sheree to tell him the truth. Sonia is stunned when the police turn up(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation into the multinational company's rise to corporate superpower, asking whether there is a dark side to the public's love affair with the firm. Former high-level insiders reveal that its customer obsession has led to a huge data-gathering operation, enabling the company to use what it knows about us to shape not only the future of retail, but the workplace and technology too. The programme also hears from senior executives who say the company is a force for good(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes becomes concerned when Dermot announces he wants to ask his childhood friend Buster, a known criminal, to be his best man. However, she is distracted when her neighbour Winnie's husband is rushed to hospital, leaving her son trying to raise funds for his wedding by getting embroiled in a money-making scheme(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The film crew arrives back in the village to discover some very sad news about a popular local resident. Meanwhile, Kerry faces some financial difficulties, so Kurtan steps in to help support her. Return of the spoof documentary, written by and starring Daisy May Cooper and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Don't Scream
programme.desc: Yung Filly hosts a game show in which three best friends go through a physical and psychological test in which they lose £100 from the £5,000 prize money every time they scream(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by singer, actor and producer Justin Timberlake and Oscar-nominated actress Anna Kendrick, who are both part of the voice cast of animated sequel Trolls World Tour. Singer-songwriter Sam Smith also appears, alongside comedian Alan Carr and Strictly and The Greatest Dancer star Oti Mabuse. Plus, more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the prestigious meeting from Emirates Arena. Singer-songwriter Sam Smith also appears, alongside comedian Alan Carr and Strictly and The Greatest Dancer star Oti Mabuse. Plus, more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Jon Kay and Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Andrew Wong and Cyrus Todiwala, and special guest John Barrowman faces his food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look back at last night's Premier League fixture between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Leicester City and preview Norwich City v Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire, while presenter Eric Knowles oversees proceedings(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the prestigious meeting from Emirates Arena. World heptathlon champion Katarina Johnson-Thompson and Scottish favourite Laura Muir are among the stars scheduled to compete, with the latter aiming to break the world record in concluding event of the day - the rarely run 1,000m. With analysis from Denise Lewis, Colin Jackson and Paula Radcliffe, and commentary by Steve Cram, Andrew Cotter and Steve Backley(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special experts edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Dr Zoe Williams, Dr Hilary Jones, Joe Swift, Rachel De Thame, Francis Wilson, Alex Beresford, Linda Barker and Judi James(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining eight acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby and Violette's relationship is pushed to the limit and Harmony's life is put in danger. Will struggles to finish a tedious report for Connie and turns to Lara for help, but he soon learns he should not mix his personal life with his work. Elsewhere, tensions run high when Ethan and Rash are forced to work together(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Norwich City v Liverpool at Carrow Road and Southampton v Burnley at Saint Mary's Stadium. Liverpool will have expected to extend their lead at the top of the table with victory over the struggling Canaries, while the much-improved Saints were looking to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of the Clarets on the opening day of the season. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton and Leicester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Roger Johnson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Norwich City v Liverpool at Carrow Road and Southampton v Burnley at Saint Mary's Stadium. Liverpool will have expected to extend their lead at the top of the table with victory over the struggling Canaries, while the much-improved Saints were looking to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of the Clarets on the opening day of the season. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton and Leicester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience at Leicester Grammar School(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Single mother Zoe from West Yorkshire longs for a new life in Australia for her and her three children, but eldest teenage daughter Courtney does not share her mother's enthusiasm(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Newmarket Racecourse, Suffolk, where two teams of best friends go head to head to maximise their returns, assisted by experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Anita also learns about a legend of horse racing who didn't set foot on the turf(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits St Mary's Kempley in Gloucestershire to see its 12th-century Bible paintings, and finds out about the Chapel of Our Lady In the Crag in North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Monmouthshire countryside to help a couple who have a love of the outdoors and dogs, and hope to buy their first home together with a £700,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are joined by Arit Anderson to create an all-year-round garden in Byfleet, Surrey, for stylish octogenarian Gay to enjoy the birds and remember her late husband. As ever, the two design teams go head to head, so it remains to be seen whther newcomer Arit's garden, which uses repeating circle motifs to create interest in an otherwise small and featureless space and incorporates a curved wooden seat surrounded by bright flowers, will triumph(n)

programme.title: Shrek: The Ghost of Lord Farquaad
programme.desc: Lord Farquaad returns as a ghost and is just as evil as ever. He kidnaps Fiona, meaning Shrek and Donkey must set out to save her - with a little help from Dragon, who ate the villainous royal in the first place. Spin-off from the Shrek movies(n)

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher meets Essex farmer David Eagle, who has let the sea inundate acres and acres of his farmland to create vital saltmarsh habitat for migratory birds(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Graham, Yasmin and Ryan join the Doctor on a trip back to 1816, where they visit the Villa Diodati on the shores of Lake Geneva as Lord Byron and the Shelleys plan a night telling ghost stories. Their aim is to spend the evening soaking up the atmosphere in the presence of some literary greats - but a supernatural presence is all too real and the Doctor is forced into a decision of earth-shattering proportions(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: After performing an excellent forceps delivery in final supervision, Dr McNulty's next patient is a woman whose husband is a drunk and abusive towards her. At the clinic, the team finally receives the long-awaited incubator for the maternity home, while Trixie is keen to support new patient Marion, who has a rare form of retinitis pigmentosa that has left her blind(n)

programme.title: Lost Lives (2019)
programme.desc: A documentary inspired by the book of the same name that records the circumstances of the deaths of every man, woman and child killed during the Troubles. With extracts read by stars of stage and screen including Roma Downey, Liam Neeson, Adrian Dunbar, Michelle Fairley, Ciaran Hinds, Bronagh Gallagher, Brendan Gleeson, Susan Lynch, Stephen Rea and Kenneth Branagh(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, which were Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park and Arsenal v Newcastle United at Emirates Stadium. The two north London clubs prevailed in the reverse fixtures on the opening weekend of the campaign and will both have been hopeful of taking the honours once again. Plus, action from yesterday and Friday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.title: Can You Beat the Bookies?
programme.desc: Comedian and football presenter Lloyd Griffith embarks on an experiment to find out if he can uncover the secrets of gambling success and the truth about the industry(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle witness the giraffes making their way to their summer paddock, while a newborn reindeer calf has become weak and is unable to suckle from its mother(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Today's auction property-developing projects take an incredible 14 days to more than two and a half years to complete. Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander visit the properties in Sandgate in Kent and Bolton in Lancashire as well as a plot of land in Enfield in north London at the start of the projects, following the progress until they're finished(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with the Walters family, who spent a trial week sampling life in Perth two years previously, to discover if they chose to stay in the UK or move to Australia(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers track down those responsible for a crash that scarred an entire community, and raid the home of an illegal money lender who preys on the vulnerable(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Liverpool's St Georges Hall, while Eric Knowles finds out why the building came to be built in the city(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma and Sid oversee an extremely complicated pregnancy, while Emma and Lena sort things out, and Daniel has an unwanted audience(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When a pampered dog runs away from a life of luxury, Frank and Lu are asked to track it down. Comedy drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a house in rural North Yorkshire on a budget of £800,000, and pauses in Harrogate to take afternoon tea(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Goat's milk ice-cream producers Ian and Lauren and cider-makers Shawn and Trudie compete with the weather and the FIFA World Cup at the Great British Food Festival(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Margie Cooper set off on a search for items in the Highlands, heading for an auction showdown in Aberdeen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: Jennifer Reoch and Joe Lindsay report from the southern Spanish city of Seville. During their stay they visit the Real Alcazar, one of the city's most impressive buildings, and the cathedral where Christopher Columbus is buried. They also enjoy a cyclo tour around Maria Luisa Park, visit a neighbourhood famous for flamenco and end up in a bathtub filled with red wine. For the short break, Jean Johansson enjoys a short getaway in East Fife(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kicking off a week of episodes for the soap's 35th anniversary, all taking place on the same day. Linda revels in her divorce from Mick but her happiness is short-lived when she comes to blows with Shirley - and things only gets worse at the Thames boat party when she continues to throw alcohol down her throat and embarrass her family. Patrick grows fed up of Isaac's behaviour towards him and pleads with Sheree to tell him the truth. Sonia is stunned when the police turn up(n)

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best concludes his journey across the province by exploring Co Tyrone's lost railway routes(n)

programme.title: PTSD - Stress of the Past
programme.desc: The stories of four people coping with post-traumatic stress disorder, told through personal videos, candid conversations and diaries and revealing the struggles they have with their mental health and how it affected those around them(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation into the multinational company's rise to corporate superpower, asking whether there is a dark side to the public's love affair with the firm. Former high-level insiders reveal that its customer obsession has led to a huge data-gathering operation, enabling the company to use what it knows about us to shape not only the future of retail, but the workplace and technology too. The programme also hears from senior executives who say the company is a force for good(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The film crew arrives back in the village to discover some very sad news about a popular local resident. Meanwhile, Kerry faces some financial difficulties, so Kurtan steps in to help support her. Return of the spoof documentary, written by and starring Daisy May Cooper and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Panzuto was a mafia boss, and a hardened killer. For years he played a key role in Naples' Camorra, but now he has turned state witness and is helping put his former associates behind bars. Dominic Casciani gains exclusive access to an Italian anti-mafia prison to meet Panzuto and hear his story of love, murder and betrayal. It's a tale which took him from the back streets of Naples to a caravan park in Blackpool. We hear from those at the sharp end of the ongoing battle against organised crime in Naples, and from those who believe the tide has finally turned. Above all we hear from the man himself: why did he decide to break with the Camorra, and what does the future hold for him?(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Panzuto was a mafia boss, and a hardened killer. For years he played a key role in Naples' Camorra, but now he has turned state witness and is helping put his former associates behind bars. Dominic Casciani gains exclusive access to an Italian anti-mafia prison to meet Panzuto and hear his story of love, murder and betrayal. It's a tale which took him from the back streets of Naples to a caravan park in Blackpool. We hear from those at the sharp end of the ongoing battle against organised crime in Naples, and from those who believe the tide has finally turned. Above all we hear from the man himself: why did he decide to break with the Camorra, and what does the future hold for him?(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: The British love chocolate, so Sian Williams investigates what makes it so irresistible and gets the public tucking into the Cadbury's chocolate bar with 30 per cent less sugar to see if it as tasty as it is healthy. Two more diets are also put to the test - the Meal Prepping for Weight-loss Diet, in which calorie-counted meals are all prepared in advance, and the Diabetes Weight-loss cookbook, road-tested by a woman who is borderline type 2 diabetic(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Oli Bell present coverage of the 3.35 Betfair Ascot Steeple Chase, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races from 2.05 and 3.15 at Haydock Park, and 2.45 at Wincanton. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Luke Harvey, Rishi Persad and Alice Plunkett, betting news from Matt Chapman, and commentary by Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of Barbados, where he prepares roasted breadfruit, salt fish and lobster, and pineapple French toast with caramelised bananas. He also visits a gully famed for lush vegetation, grapefruits and green monkeys, and catches up with an old friend who runs a beachside snack shop out of a converted bus(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up their version of classic shepherd's pie, a simple recipe for fudgy chocolate cookies, sushi with two different fillings and a bakewell tart. Their guest is chef and baker Sal Dhalla, who cooks her roasted vegetable farinata(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia reaches the island of Chios, where her family's story began. She's joined by her mother Chrissi and together they explore the home of their ancestors. In search of the fascinating history of Mastica, a natural resin only found on Chios, they visit a farm to discover how this mystical and miraculous tree sap has been harvested for thousands of years. After following in the footsteps of legendary Greek poet Homer, who is reputed to have been born on the island, their trip comes to an emotional end when they track down the house where Julia's great-grandparents once lived(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: All hands are on deck as the metro system prepares for the busiest day of the year - the Great North Run. This year, a new and untested station in South Shields is at the centre of things. One of the event's regular runners prepares for a personal return to the course, as he runs in tribute to a friend(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the penultimate round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' will.i.am, Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Meghan Trainor seek out the nation's best vocal talent. The coveted spaces on the coaches' teams are filling up and as ever, there is a simple twist to the auditions - with the four coaches selecting their teams without being able to see them, looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as 2018 I'm a Celebrity campmates Rita Simons, Malique Thompson-Dwyer, Fleur East and James McVey try to win as much money as they dare before teaming up in the final round against one of the show's five quiz experts. But which Chaser will they be facing tonight?(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners take to the ice once more, hoping they can do enough to impress judges Christopher Dean, Jayne Torvill, Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman and avoid a place in the bottom two. Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby present the action, including a musical interlude from the Script, accompanying a routine by the professional skaters(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: On the final leg of their journey, the pair continue through the snow to New Hampshire. Here, they meet rally-driving champion Tim O'Neil who gives them a crash course in driving in the ice and snow before they take on an intense time trial. The pair travel on to Maine, where they take part in some wilderness training, before bidding farewell to the RV and heading out into the extremely choppy seas for some lobster fishing. Before heading home, the pair look back on some of their favourite moments from the trip(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp talks about his life and career as one of the most respected and experienced football managers in the modern game, as well as his victorious I'm a Celebrity appearance in 2018, when he was crowned King of the Jungle. He discusses the many twists and turns of his rollercoaster life, including a road accident in 1990 in which his friend Brian Tiler died. Harry also opens up about a dramatic dawn raid on his house and the death threats he received when he became manager of Southampton(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The documentary going behind the scenes of the London airport returns for a fifth season. Inside the terminal, seasoned pro Demi dishes out deals for delayed passengers, while outside on the airfield, Ian is on the runway as it goes into lockdown with a full armed police presence. For the first time this series, viewers will also see the work of the Border Force's canine drugs detection team - Rachel and her dog Cole, who in his career has intercepted more than £1million worth of class-A contraband. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: Teach My Pet to Do That
programme.desc: Animal lover, comedian and presenter Alexander Armstrong opens the doors of his multispecies pet training school to welcome Ethel the goat and Fagin the crow, in the hope they can learn to tidy up their toys and put them away in a box. The week's star pupil is Smurf, a Parson Russell terrier who holds the world record of performing the most tricks in a minute. The trainers hit the road to teach an Arab mare called Ruby Ribena to count in the hope she can help turn a little girl's party into a fairy tale(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh asks the questions as contestants Mick, Roberta, Danny and Katie pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vanessa is left desperate, Charity is disheartened, and Dawn hides her worries(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: The financial journalist and Angellica Bell offer advice to viewers about consumer rights when buying goods in store and online, and look at what the best mortgage rates are for people who are navigating the property market?(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ali calls at Maria's flat and begs her to give their relationship a chance, convinced she feels the same way about him. Gemma tells Chesney that Frescho will have to reschedule the photoshoot as Aled's doctor's appointment is far more important, and it looks like Ed may discover Danny and James's relationship(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam and Karen's future is thrown into jeopardy by an unexpected offer, and David is in turmoil over his growing feelings for Robyn. While Jenny braces herself for her first mammogram after her treatment for cancer, Pete helps their son Adam realise his dream. John Thomson, James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst star in the concluding instalment of the ninth season of Mike Bullen's long-running comedy drama, with guest appearances by Frances Barber and Paul Ritter(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: The BRITs at 40
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall narrates a look back at the history of the awards ceremony, featuring archive footage of some of the most memorable moments, from Oasis and Blur's rivalry to Stormzy's attack on the government, and classic performances by Adele, Rihanna and Drake. The programme also recalls less successful moments, from the infamous 1989 ceremony hosted by Mick Fleetwood and Sam Fox, and Madonna struggles with her cape. Featuring interviews with stars including Robbie Williams, Mel B, Mick Fleetwood, Little Mix, Sam Smith, Ellie Goulding, Sharon Osbourne, Bros, Dua Lipa, Rick Astley and Tom Jones(n)

programme.title: Gary Rhodes: The First Rock Star Chef
programme.desc: Documentary paying tribute to the celebrity chef who died in 2019, exploring his impact on both British food and television and showcasing footage from an incomplete final series. Featuring contributions from friends and family, including Gordon Ramsay, Tom Kerridge, Alex Ferguson, and Gary's sons, George and Sam(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: The British love chocolate, so Sian Williams investigates what makes it so irresistible and gets the public tucking into the Cadbury's chocolate bar with 30 per cent less sugar to see if it as tasty as it is healthy. Two more diets are also put to the test - the Meal Prepping for Weight-loss Diet, in which calorie-counted meals are all prepared in advance, and the Diabetes Weight-loss cookbook, road-tested by a woman who is borderline type 2 diabetic(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Oli Bell present coverage of the 3.35 Betfair Ascot Steeple Chase, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.00. Plus, races from 2.05 and 3.15 at Haydock Park, and 2.45 at Wincanton. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Luke Harvey, Rishi Persad and Alice Plunkett, betting news from Matt Chapman, and commentary by Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the show's most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shepherd, in which the the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the grand final of the singing contest. Only three of the show's famous contestants have kept their identity a secret, and tonight, these three finalists will be competing for the series trophy before all of them are unmasked. Regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Ken Jeong will have one last chance to guess the famous faces hidden behind the bizarre costumes, before all 12 of the show's performers take to the stage together for a special group performance(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the penultimate round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' will.i.am, Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Meghan Trainor seek out the nation's best vocal talent. The coveted spaces on the coaches' teams are filling up and as ever, there is a simple twist to the auditions - with the four coaches selecting their teams without being able to see them, looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.desc: The ageing fighter contemplates a return to the ring when a computer simulation reveals he would have defeated the current world heavyweight champion Mason 'The Line' Dixon, who responds by challenging Rocky to face him for real. programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of Barbados, where he prepares roasted breadfruit, salt fish and lobster, and pineapple French toast with caramelised bananas. He also visits a gully famed for lush vegetation, grapefruits and green monkeys, and catches up with an old friend who runs a beachside snack shop out of a converted bus(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up their version of classic shepherd's pie, a simple recipe for fudgy chocolate cookies, sushi with two different fillings and a bakewell tart. Their guest is chef and baker Sal Dhalla, who cooks her roasted vegetable farinata(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia reaches the island of Chios, where her family's story began. She's joined by her mother Chrissi and together they explore the home of their ancestors. In search of the fascinating history of Mastica, a natural resin only found on Chios, they visit a farm to discover how this mystical and miraculous tree sap has been harvested for thousands of years. After following in the footsteps of legendary Greek poet Homer, who is reputed to have been born on the island, their trip comes to an emotional end when they track down the house where Julia's great-grandparents once lived(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: All hands are on deck as the metro system prepares for the busiest day of the year - the Great North Run. This year, a new and untested station in South Shields is at the centre of things. One of the event's regular runners prepares for a personal return to the course, as he runs in tribute to a friend(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the penultimate round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' will.i.am, Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Meghan Trainor seek out the nation's best vocal talent. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as 2018 I'm a Celebrity campmates Rita Simons, Malique Thompson-Dwyer, Fleur East and James McVey try to win as much money as they dare before teaming up in the final round against one of the show's five quiz experts. But which Chaser will they be facing tonight?(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners take to the ice once more, hoping they can do enough to impress judges Christopher Dean, Jayne Torvill, Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman and avoid a place in the bottom two. Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby present the action, including a musical interlude from the Script, accompanying a routine by the professional skaters(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: On the final leg of their journey, the pair continue through the snow to New Hampshire. Here, they meet rally-driving champion Tim O'Neil who gives them a crash course in driving in the ice and snow before they take on an intense time trial. The pair travel on to Maine, where they take part in some wilderness training, before bidding farewell to the RV and heading out into the extremely choppy seas for some lobster fishing. Before heading home, the pair look back on some of their favourite moments from the trip(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp talks about his life and career as one of the most respected and experienced football managers in the modern game, as well as his victorious I'm a Celebrity appearance in 2018, when he was crowned King of the Jungle. He discusses the many twists and turns of his rollercoaster life, including a road accident in 1990 in which his friend Brian Tiler died. Harry also opens up about a dramatic dawn raid on his house and the death threats he received when he became manager of Southampton(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The documentary going behind the scenes of the London airport returns for a fifth season. Inside the terminal, seasoned pro Demi dishes out deals for delayed passengers, while outside on the airfield, Ian is on the runway as it goes into lockdown with a full armed police presence. For the first time this series, viewers will also see the work of the Border Force's canine drugs detection team - Rachel and her dog Cole, who in his career has intercepted more than £1million worth of class-A contraband. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: Teach My Pet to Do That
programme.desc: Animal lover, comedian and presenter Alexander Armstrong opens the doors of his multispecies pet training school to welcome Ethel the goat and Fagin the crow, in the hope they can learn to tidy up their toys and put them away in a box. The week's star pupil is Smurf, a Parson Russell terrier who holds the world record of performing the most tricks in a minute. The trainers hit the road to teach an Arab mare called Ruby Ribena to count in the hope she can help turn a little girl's party into a fairy tale(n)

But they must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill presents a review of the show's most comical clips, including amazing accidents, cute kids and funny animals(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Kandi, now a successful Hollywood actress, tries to get back with Alan. He turns her down, citing his commitment to Lindsay, before landing himself in hot water when compromising images appear online. Award-winning American comedy series, starring Jon Cryer, Ashton Kutcher, Angus T Jones and Conchata Ferrell(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden feels that being a billionaire clouds his interaction with the ladies, so he pretends to be an ordinary guy and immediately finds success with saleswoman Kate. Alan goes along with the masquerade, pretending to be rich, but the power goes to his head(n)

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Laura Whitmore presents as the final week of the relationship-based reality show continues with more surprises in store as the singletons compete to pair off in Cape Town(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Aftersun
programme.desc: Laura Whitmore is joined by Kem Cetinay, Arielle Free and last year's winner Amber Gill for all the latest gossip and news from South Africa(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bethany and Daniel hunt for evidence against Ray, Emma and Seb offer Alina a place to stay, and Gary inadvertently drives Maria into Ali's arms(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When the body of an elderly woman is discovered at a well-known suicide spot, Ronnie soon detects foul play, with suspicion falling on the victim's son and daughter-in-law, as well as a doctor who has recently arrived in the UK. However, even after a confession is secured, Joe remains baffled as to the motive, and Kate struggles to remain professional - jeopardising her career and leaving Jake to prosecute the case alone. Guest starring Haydn Gwynne(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A blood-spattered hotel room and a stolen credit card lead Ronnie and Joe to Charles Hutton, a member of the wealthy upper classes who dotes on his wife Camille and daughter Georgia. The detectives find clues that suggest Georgia gave birth at the scene, but where is the baby now? The young woman and her boyfriend Rufus prove difficult for Jake and Kate to prosecute, and to make matters worse, the defence counsel turns out to be Jake's old nemesis. As the evidence stacks up, how far will Charles go to protect his privileged family? Guest starring James Wilby and Joseph Millson(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a series of shootings claims one of their own, Ronnie and Joe have little time to grieve - they must solve the case before the killer reaches their next target on a hit list that includes CPS director Henry Sharpe. It gradually becomes apparent that the common thread connecting all the names is the case of Mark Glendon, convicted of the murder of his wife four years earlier. Although he's still in custody, a retrial is imminent, putting pressure on the team to catch the murderer before the jury delivers its verdict(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old Susan Webb is desperate find the daughter she gave up for adoption in 1968. At the time, she was suffering with depression, and after being treated with electroconvulsive therapy that severely affected her memory, she was left with no recollections of her child. Meanwhile, Sonia Walsh looks for the mother that abandoned her at the age of three with her younger sister. During her investigations, she discovered her mother had also given up another baby, and she is now determined to make up for the pain of the past. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is confident she can settle a mix-up over Robert's birthday(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bethany and Daniel hunt for evidence against Ray, Emma and Seb offer Alina a place to stay, and Gary inadvertently drives Maria into Ali's arms(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A custody battle begins, Matty is in danger up at the farm and Vanessa argues with Charity over what she has done. Moira gets a shock when Cain visits(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A disused abbey that is believed to be cursed is about to be turned into a pub, a decision that has angered many superstitious locals - not to mention the real ale society. The day after the launch night, the curse appears to have claimed a victim - a body is found boiled to death in a kettle of beer, in a manner very similar to an infamous execution on the site in the 16th century. Elaine Paige guest stars, while Annette Badland joins the cast as new pathologist Fleur Perkins(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: It's turnaround day on the Regal Princess, when the crew has to help 3,300 passengers check out with 10,000 pieces of luggage and then welcome the new batch - with just a few hours to clean and prepare 1,800 rooms. As the staff members work against the clock, hotel manager Dirk makes his usual inspections - and will not tolerate any slip in standards. Meanwhile, dancer Dulcie is feeling homesick, and front-desk supervisor Herbert is under pressure to locate some missing luggage and have it delivered at the next port(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The staff compete to win the coveted title of employee of the month, which carries a top prize of $500, as well as deck privileges, tours and extra time off. Deck hand Rhymond is among those with their eye on the top prize, having braved the elements since 5am to keep the ship looking pristine. Meanwhile, Timothy feels sad about leaving his friends on the front desk to start his new job with the shore excursions team, and engineer Scott is left in charge when his boss goes on leave, with jobs including making drinking water from the sea(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna meets the Maharaja of Dungarpur who shows her around his lakeside palace, and a Dalit community in Gujarat, considered to be India's lowest caste. Braving the roads of Mumbai, Joanna takes a ride in the city's only all-female taxi company and visits the Times of India, where her uncle was editor of the paper in the 1930s and 40s. She then overcomes her fear of heights to explore the World One Tower, soon to be Mumbai's tallest luxury residential building(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Gabriel joins Ruby on an outreach programme to a tea plantation, but discovers that she has not been completely honest about her reasons for wanting to go there. Back at the clinic, Lydia tries to help a patient who she believes is a victim of domestic abuse(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby is still reeling from her discoveries at the tea plantation, and must decide whether she can forgive past mistakes. Meanwhile, Mari goes head to head with Lydia when a surrogate mother gives birth in the hospital. Medical drama about a junior doctor who starts a new life in a South Indian hospital run by an eccentric English ex-pat. Starring Amrita Acharia, Nimmi Harasgama, Amanda Redman, James Krishna Floyd, Neil Morrissey, Darshan Jariwalla, Philip Jackson, Achint Kaur and Mandeep Dhillon(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Still reeling from the death of one of their own, Ronnie and Joe face a new challenge when an innocent young mum is fatally stabbed in a busy marketplace, just as a senior police officer publicly pledges to crack down on knife crime. Under pressure from all sides, the pair struggle to gather evidence against the youth they think is responsible, and the suspect's unintentional confession appears to bring the case to a close. However, Ronnie is the only person who hears the admission, and it proves difficult getting anyone to believe him. With the detective's honesty cast into doubt and his effectiveness questioned at every turn, is this the end of his career? With Tony Gardner, Sharon Small and Colin Salmon(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: In the wake of the fall of France to the Germans, guests flood into the hotel, including one who catches Lady Hamilton's eye - the Comte de St Claire. Peggy worries about Billy's enthusiasm to sign up as soon as he turns 18, but knows there is little she can do to protect him when his mind looks like it's made up to join his father's regiment, infamously used as canon fodder. When Max faces hostility from the staff, Emma goes out on a limb to defend him - and immediately regrets it(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A custody battle begins, Matty is in danger up at the farm and Vanessa argues with Charity over what she has done. Moira gets a shock when Cain visits(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera skips work to clean for Curly, and David brings on Sally's contractions. Deirdre and Samir spend a romantic day together, and Steve realises he has been used by Mike(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Websters are delighted with their baby daughter. Alma throws Steve out of the cafe when he starts talking about Mike conning him, and the Wiltons are united in their grief(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the day of the Home Farm hunt and Rachel organises the hunt saboteurs. The village gossips about Zoe's sexuality, and Linda tells her she cannot work for her anymore(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel receives a shock, while Frank and Ned argue in the Woolpack about Ned's attitude to Zoe. The village committee decide to hold a memorial service for the air crash victims(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently released ex-convict who served time for the rape of a local girl becomes the target of a hate campaign - prompting Rob to point the finger of suspicion at the victim's family. The bobby is shocked when old acquaintance DS Rachel Dawson arrives in Aidensfield on the trail of an escaped prisoner, while Peggy accidentally knocks down an old friend with her car. Jonathan Kerrigan stars(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The family warms to the French way of life, but Charley is unhappy with Mariette's flirting - and after a little Dutch courage, decides to take matters into his own hands. Drama, starring Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert decides that Angie, Ida and himself should all go on a diet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian trawlerman found hiding in a local woman's shed is accused of being a spy by a Home Office official (The Office star Patrick Baladi) - but Walker and Carol discover the government worker has secrets of his own to hide. Peggy turns detective after an old note found among a dead friend's belongings raises questions about her husband's disappearance. With Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of the Home Farm Classic Car Rally arrives. Rachel's pregnancy is confirmed, Kathy tells Bernard she thinks she is cracking up, and Eric takes revenge on Frank(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe asks Chris what his intentions are towards Rachel, who tells him about the pregnancy. Eric tries to reassure Alan about his financial worries(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samir is shocked by Deirdre's proposal, Raquel suggests Curly should help out at the Rovers, and Sally is upset that Rita has not been to visit the new baby yet(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Wiltons celebrate their sixth wedding anniversary, and Mavis is thrilled when Derek gives her a new budgie. The Websters decide to change Lauren's name to Sophie(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian trawlerman found hiding in a local woman's shed is accused of being a spy by a Home Office official (The Office star Patrick Baladi) - but Walker and Carol discover the government worker has secrets of his own to hide. Peggy turns detective after an old note found among a dead friend's belongings raises questions about her husband's disappearance. With Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser(n)

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The detective interrogates a mysterious Dane who has been caught with the dead body of a diamond dealer in his car, and who proves entirely resistant to his questioning. Maigret is convinced his suspect's sister may know something, but soon discovers that everyone in the small rural town where they live is hiding something. Guest starring Tom Wlaschiha (Game of Thrones) and Kevin McNally (the Pirates of the Caribbean movies)(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Comedy drama following an American police chief as he moves to the UK and joins the Lincolnshire Police Force. High-flying police officer Bill Hixon arrives in Boston, Lincolnshire to start a new life with his 14-year-old daughter in an effort to escape painful memories in their recent past. However, Bill is not welcomed by his new team, as his deputry Lydia Price makes no secret of her disdain for him, and local journalist Lisa Cranston grills him on his approach. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.desc: Joanna meets the Maharaja of Dungarpur who shows her around his lakeside palace, and a Dalit community in Gujarat, considered to be India's lowest caste. Braving the roads of Mumbai, Joanna takes a ride in the city's only all-female taxi company and visits the Times of India, where her uncle was editor of the paper in the 1930s and 40s. She then overcomes her fear of heights to explore the World One Tower, soon to be Mumbai's tallest luxury residential building(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Gabriel joins Ruby on an outreach programme to a tea plantation, but discovers that she has not been completely honest about her reasons for wanting to go there. Back at the clinic, Lydia tries to help a patient who she believes is a victim of domestic abuse(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby is still reeling from her discoveries at the tea plantation, and must decide whether she can forgive past mistakes. Meanwhile, Mari goes head to head with Lydia when a surrogate mother gives birth in the hospital. Medical drama about a junior doctor who starts a new life in a South Indian hospital run by an eccentric English ex-pat. Starring Amrita Acharia, Nimmi Harasgama, Amanda Redman, James Krishna Floyd, Neil Morrissey, Darshan Jariwalla, Philip Jackson, Achint Kaur and Mandeep Dhillon(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Still reeling from the death of one of their own, Ronnie and Joe face a new challenge when an innocent young mum is fatally stabbed in a busy marketplace, just as a senior police officer publicly pledges to crack down on knife crime. Under pressure from all sides, the pair struggle to gather evidence against the youth they think is responsible, and the suspect's unintentional confession appears to bring the case to a close. However, Ronnie is the only person who hears the admission, and it proves difficult getting anyone to believe him. With the detective's honesty cast into doubt and his effectiveness questioned at every turn, is this the end of his career? With Tony Gardner, Sharon Small and Colin Salmon(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: In the wake of the fall of France to the Germans, guests flood into the hotel, including one who catches Lady Hamilton's eye - the Comte de St Claire. Peggy worries about Billy's enthusiasm to sign up as soon as he turns 18, but knows there is little she can do to protect him when his mind looks like it's made up to join his father's regiment, infamously used as canon fodder. When Max faces hostility from the staff, Emma goes out on a limb to defend him - and immediately regrets it(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A custody battle begins, Matty is in danger up at the farm and Vanessa argues with Charity over what she has done. Moira gets a shock when Cain visits(n)

programme.desc: The detective interrogates a mysterious Dane who has been caught with the dead body of a diamond dealer in his car, and who proves entirely resistant to his questioning. Maigret is convinced his suspect's sister may know something, but soon discovers that everyone in the small rural town where they live is hiding something. Guest starring Tom Wlaschiha (Game of Thrones) and Kevin McNally (the Pirates of the Caribbean movies)(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Comedy drama following an American police chief as he moves to the UK and joins the Lincolnshire Police Force. High-flying police officer Bill Hixon arrives in Boston, Lincolnshire to start a new life with his 14-year-old daughter in an effort to escape painful memories in their recent past. However, Bill is not welcomed by his new team, as his deputry Lydia Price makes no secret of her disdain for him, and local journalist Lisa Cranston grills him on his approach. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: Snowdon gradually falls silent in the winter months and the residents around the highest peak in England and Wales have it almost to themselves. programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: In 1592, Elizabeth I visited Sudeley Castle in the Cotswolds for a three-day party commemorating the nation's victory over the Spanish Armada. Here, Raksha Dave, Alex Langlands and assorted volunteers and archaeologists hunt for a lost garden planted as part of the celebrations, while there is also a visit to Kenilworth Castle to investigate an Elizabethan garden still in full bloom today. Plus, a look at how the garden's Tudor owner, Robert Dudley transformed his estate to win favour with his Queen(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Apocalypse - Death of a Dynasty

programme.title: Pompeii: The New Revelations

Dan squeezes into a network of illegally dug tunnels that burrow into a huge unexcavated villa, while Raksha is given privileged access to the highly restricted Regio V area of the town(n)

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell heads to the industrial heartlands of north-east England, beginning on Wearside, where he learns about the world's first railway designed for steam locomotives. Rob also visits the ‘living' museum at Beamish and rides a railway from George Stephenson's day, following an old line into Hartlepool, where a medieval port was turned into a mega-centre of coal, rail and shipping in just one generation(n)

programme.title: Building the Ultimate
programme.desc: A look at the technologies of the MSC Bellissima, one of the world's largest cruise ships. This documentary follows Bellissima on a single eight-day journey around Spain, France, Italy and Malta. The voyage begins at Barcelona and moves on to Marseille, Genoa, Naples, Messina and Valletta. The ship's super-sized steel box construction allows it to sail high in the water and to slip into the shallowest ports, while precision-engineered propellers made from a super corrosion-resistant alloy thrust it forwards with minimal noise, so the 7,000 passengers and crew can fully enjoy the trip(n)

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks(n)

programme.desc: Action from the 10th round of fixtures, featuring Gloucester v Exeter Chiefs at Kingsholm, Leicester Tigers v Wasps at Welford Road, Harlequins v London Irish at Twickenham Stoop, Saracens v Sale Sharks at Allianz Park, Worcester Warriors v Bath at Sixways Stadium and Northampton Saints v Bristol Bears at Franklin's Gardens(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An insight into the working life of officers patrolling the nation's roads, using modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice(n)

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Duncan face their most important mission to date as the vessel takes part in Operation Inherent Resolve, which aims to keep two Russian warships away from a French aircraft carrier so its jets can safely carry out airstrikes on the nearby remnants of Iisi in Syria and Iraq(n)

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell tells the dramatic story of the Second World War aircraft carrier, which hunted down some of Nazi Germany's most feared warships(n)

programme.title: Fury (2014)
programme.desc: A veteran American sergeant leads a tank crew on one final dangerous mission in the last months of the Second World War. The assignment takes the soldiers behind enemy lines and into battle with overwhelming enemy forces, while the presence of an inexperienced new recruit in the team raises tension. Drama, starring Brad Pitt and Shia LaBeouf(n)

programme.title: Stuart Little (1999)
programme.desc: An American couple decide their son needs a brother, and plan to adopt another child - but when they get to the orphanage they are greeted by a charming talking mouse named Stuart, who fits the bill perfectly. However, their jealous cat hatches a plot to get rid of the new arrival. Comedy adventure, starring Geena Davis and Hugh Laurie, with the voice of Michael J Fox(n)

programme.title: Enemy Mine (1985)
programme.desc: In the midst of a war between humans and aliens, two space pilots from opposite sides are marooned together on an inhospitable planet and are forced to set aside their mutual animosity to survive. Sci-fi adventure, starring Dennis Quaid, Louis Gossett Jr and Brion James(n)

programme.title: Megamind (2010)
programme.desc: An evil genius finally succeeds in killing the superhero who has foiled his every evil scheme, but finds life without an enemy boring. To fill the gap, he transforms an ordinary man into a worthy adversary - but when his creation turns evil, it falls to the villain to save the world. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Ferrell, Tina Fey and Brad Pitt(n)

programme.title: A Thousand Words (2012)
programme.desc: A spiritual guru decides to teach his lying literary agent a lesson for his dishonesty, and places a curse on him. The spell means he can only speak another 1,000 words in his life - and when the words run out, he will die. The only way to break the enchantment is to put right all his past mistakes. Comedy, starring Eddie Murphy and Kerry Washington(n)

programme.title: Snow White & the Huntsman (2012)

However, he comes to realise his mistress's evil ways, and instead trains his intended victim to fight so they can bring the tyrant's rule to an end. Fantasy adventure, with Kristen Stewart, Chris Hemsworth and Charlize Theron(n)

programme.title: Arrival (2016)
programme.desc: The world is thrown into uproar when 12 alien spaceships arrive at locations around the globe. As tensions rise between nations and fear of the arrivals grows, a recently bereaved linguist is recruited by the US army to find a way to communicate with the aliens and discover why they have come. Sci-fi drama, starring Amy Adams, Jeremy Renner and Forest Whitaker(n)

programme.title: The Midnight Man (2016)

Action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Sung Kang and Christian Slater(n)

Animated comedy based on the TV series, with the voices of Elizabeth Daily, Christine Cavanaugh, Kath Soucie, Whoopi Goldberg and Busta Rhymes(n)

Family comedy sequel, starring Zachary Gordon and Steve Zahn(n)

Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover(n)

The situation rapidly gets out of control, leading to the worst environmental disaster in US history. Fact-based drama, starring Mark Wahlberg, Kurt Russell and John Malkovich(n)

However, the plan goes awry and the two guinea pigs awake to find themselves in the year 2505, where they are the most intelligent people in the world and must battle to save the human race from its own stupidity. Sci-fi comedy, with Luke Wilson, Maya Rudolph and Dax Shepard(n)

Andrei Tarkovsky's sci-fi drama, starring Aleksandr Kaydanovskiy, Anatoliy Solonitsyn and Nikolay Grinko. In Russian(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three petrolheads demonstrate their love of small cars with a trip to Ukraine. Jeremy Clarkson chooses a Volkswagen Up for the journey, while Richard Hammond takes a Ford Fiesta and James May opts for a Dacia Sandero. On their adventure, the trio absorb the history of the Crimean Peninsula, including a visit to an old submarine pen - but the carefree exploration looks likely to become a chore when Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to a long drive north through the expansive country, during which they must combat serious boredom. And as if that wasn't arduous enough, a new task is set when they arrive in Kiev - a trip into the wasteland of Chernobyl. This edition also features a track test of the Danish Zenvo ST1 supercar and James Blunt returns for another drive in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan sets out on another international odyssey to meet members of his extended family. This time, he meet relatives who left Sri Lanka and settled in the USA, and by visiting them in Florida, Romesh hopes to get a taste of what his life could have been like if his parents had opted to migrate to the US instead of the UK. The first edition focuses on the Channel Island of Guernsey, which Hitler occupied and fortified over the course of five years(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of three preserved steam railway lines - the South Devon Railway heritage line, the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway and the Great Central Railway. Sports scientist Megan Smith has a dream of tending the fire of a steam locomotive on the Ffestiniog & Welsh Highland Railway, and she stokes the bolier of NG143 and sets off along the 25 miles from Caernarfon to Porthmadog(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates the opportunities tides create for marine life, showing giant stingrays gliding on currents for a rest, South African sea snails surfing the waves in pursuit of a meal, crabs playing football on a beach, and bottlenose dolphins embarking on a dangerous game of catch. In the oceanic world each turn of the tide can mean life or death for its inhabitants(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Exploring the wealth of marine life that inhabits the ocean's edge, dealing with the dangers of an ever-shifting environment. David Attenborough observes nesting seabirds in east Russia, Patagonian killer whales, Newfoundland caplin fish, walruses, and Brazilian green turtles - which make their annual expedition to an island in the middle of the Atlantic to lay their eggs(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the documentary exploring Earth's natural history and the factors that have affected it. The first edition examines how the sun affects life on Earth through the seasons, following a polar bear preparing its cubs for a long journey to avoid becoming stranded on floating glaciers. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)
