programme.desc: Chris travels from Bangkok in Thailand to Mandalay in Myanmar, tracing the route of the notorious Burma-Siam Railway. The 250-mile line was built by the Japanese during the Second World War using enslaved Asian workers and Allied PoWs, and Chris visits the famous site of the Bridge on the River Kwai along the way(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time(n)

programme.title: The British Middle Class
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Tristram Hunt examines the social class's origins. He discovers how the backlash against the landed aristocracy began with a group of radical thinkers who craved progress and used their money from industry to begin a process of urban enlightenment. However, the growth of mass production led the newly moneyed leaders to move further away from the factories, beginning the trend for suburban living as a mark of status - though it meant the middle classes turning their back on where they came from(n)

programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The infamous Borgia family was devastated when favourite son Juan was murdered in 1497. Here, modern experts set about cracking the case and fingering the killer(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The untold secret story of war production that shaped the Second World War, beginning with how the German aviation industry came to dominate the skies of Europe(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The comedian makes five train journeys taking in the spectacular sights of Africa, travelling to unexpected places in deserts and forests, villages and cities, and finding out how the railways are used today. He begins in the north, on a line that links the trading cities of Morocco, Algeria and Tunisia. Childhood sweethearts Jess and Ella are already lost, while in HQ, the hunters believe 78-year-old grandfather Mervyn will be an easy target - until they uncover his secret past(n)

programme.title: Sex Tape
programme.desc: Three couples look for solutions to their sex and intimacy issues as this revealing series continues. Ray and Diana, TJ and Tiffany, and Sam and Stevie film their most private moments over one week, before sharing their sex tapes with the group and with relationship therapist Anjula Mutanda. In this instalment, one couple's unusual fetish divides opinion(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Monty Ravenscroft and Claire Loewe, who are planning to build an experimental home on a small plot of land in Peckham, south London. Unable to afford a conventional house, the pair envisage a finished product that is a miracle of space-age design, complete with sliding-glass roof lights and mezzanine bedroom pods(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at BnB at 43 in Richmond, North Yorkshire, where Jenny and Johnny Hatch hope their guests will enjoy a home-from-home experience(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: A&E doctor Sukhi Bhullar, support worker Louise Woodward-Styles, boxer Shinny Davenport and HR manager Fiona Morris host dinner parties in Salford for a chance to win the £1,000 prize. The week includes high-minded conversation, probing questions and a Jewish-style Friday-night dinner(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is elated to be signed up by a modelling agency, convinced fame and wealth are just around the corner - but his dreams of success prove short-lived. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A stranger reveals to the Barones that Frank used to be his mentor - much to the bemusement of Ray and Robert, who find it hard to believe their father could ever have inspired anyone. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Peter Boyle and Brad Garrett(n)

programme.desc: The shrink bumps into a former friend of Martin's while visiting Maris in hospital. The pair parted on bad terms, so Frasier tries to effect a reconciliation. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A plumber hired to repair Frasier's toilet turns out to be the bully who made Niles' life a misery at school. Comedy, guest starring John C McGinley, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A family from Teesside club together to buy a rundown property to turn into a restaurant in Spain, while a couple from Maidstone take on a cycling tour business in Girona(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay travels to the El Greco restaurant in Austin, Texas, where the owners have resorted to using a microwave to prepare their 'authentic' Greek dishes. Gordon faces the difficult task of uniting a squabbling family before tackling the bigger problem of a business on the brink of collapse(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The fly-on-the-cage-wall documentary returns after a Christmas break. Dominant chimpanzee Dylan is distracted by the females, giving a new generation of males the opportunity to challenge the pecking order. Pudu couple Serena and Odin have a new arrival - but big brother Lucas seems intent on terrorising his new sister - and there is a battle for survival in the aquarium among the Indian mudskipper fish, who can exist both in and out of water(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: The restaurant of love throws opens its doors at the most romantic time of the year, as maitre d' Fred Sirieix and his staff hope to help more singletons find their perfect match. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In north London, Harrow council officers investigate an overcrowded property. An inspection uncovers many issues, including an unauthorised loft conversion that is a danger to the tenants who sleep there. A couple in Suffolk agree to rent a property to its former owner, who now refuses to leave, and a single mum tricks her way into a Milton Keynes property, confirming she has paid the deposit having done no such thing(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The story of landlords Pauline and Simon, who bought a property blind with tenants in situ - not realising the difficulties that would ensue. It turned out their tenants were causing havoc in the neighbourhood and had more than a hundred arrests between them for drugs and domestic violence - and if the landlords did not evict them, they themselves could be prosecuted. Plus, a Slough landlord forced to call in an eviction expert when his tenant cut off all communication(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: The game show returns in a midweek slot, with a host of fresh games designed to test contestants' skill, nerve and determination. First up is Derry-Londonderry radiographer Lindsey, who believes that working in the trauma department of Southampton Hospital will help her stay calm under pressure. She is followed by retired Yorkshireman Keith, who at 70 is the oldest person to have appeared on the show. Will his extra years give him an advantage in defeating the Cube? Phillip Schofield hosts(n)

programme.title: Take Your Pick
programme.desc: Des O'Connor hosts the popular game show, inviting competitors to play the Yes-No game and then make that all-important final choice - whether to take the money or open the box(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap show(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Veteran newsreader Alastair Stewart, Atomic Kitten's Natasha Hamilton, TV cleaner Kim Woodburn and presenter Keith Chegwin compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Former Blur bassist Alex James, ex-MP Ann Widdecombe, Radio 1 DJ Sara Cox and This Morning's Eamonn Holmes pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a large cash prize for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught. Bradley Walsh presents(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Guests taking their place in the hotseat are Greg Rusedski and his wife Lucy playing for the Catholic Children's Society, Alastair Campbell and his partner Fiona Millar playing for the Leukaemia Research Fund and Joan Collins and husband Percy Gibson playing for the Shooting Star Children's Hospice. Chris Tarrant presents(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Former Dancing on Ice judge Karen Barber and comedian Paddy McGuinness are joined by their families as they compete to win up to £30,000 for their chosen charities, Cancer Research and the British Heart Foundation. Hosted by Vernon Kay(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a EUFA European Championship-themed edition of the general knowledge quiz, marking the beginning of the Euro 2016 competition on Friday. The competing duos are Welsh national side defender Danny Gabbidon and Scrum V presenter Jason Mohammad, and ex-Bolton Wanderers manager Neil Lennon and former Premier League forward Iain Dowie. BBC Sport's Gabby Logan partners Steve Wilson, and Sunderland Ladies striker Stephanie Roche teams up with retired player Kevin Kilbane, both of whom have represented the Republic of Ireland on the international stage(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Former Dancing on Ice judges Jason Gardiner and Ashley Roberts, Birds of a Feather actress Linda Robson and actor and TV presenter Christopher Biggins compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: West End stars Jodie Prenger and Gareth Gates compete to win cash for their chosen charities, which include Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Bobby Moore Fund. The pair are joined by their families and £30,000 is up for grabs as Vernon Kay asks a series of questions(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay presents a festive battle between two of ITV1's biggest soap families, hoping to win up to £30,000 for a charity of their choice. Coronation Street's Michael Le Vell (Kevin Webster) teams up with screen wife Sally Whittaker (Sally), Helen Flanagan and Brooke Vincent (who play daughters Rosie and Sophie) and Peter Armitage (Kevin's dad Bill) to play for Genesis Breast Cancer. They face Emmerdale's King family, with Nick Miles (Jimmy King) joined by Nicola Wheeler (partner Nicola), Tom Lister (brother Carl), Kelsey-Beth Crossley (half-sister Scarlett) and Lucy Pargeter (Carl's on-off lover Chas Dingle). Their charities are St Anne's Community Services and Safe at Last(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Former athlete Colin Jackson and broadcaster Esther Rantzen are joined by their families as they compete to win up to £30,000 for their chosen causes, Cardiff-based youth charity Fairbridge and ChildLine respectively. Hosted by Vernon Kay(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Coronation Street's Antony Cotton (Sean Tully), co-host of The One Show Alex Jones, writer and broadcaster Gyles Brandreth and Flog It! presenter Paul Martin compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)
